entries.fix_rationale: Upgrades the directly used HTML sanitizer from the vulnerable 1.x release to a maintained 2.x release containing XSS bypass fixes while keeping the existing sanitizeHtmlLib(html) call pattern.
entries.functionality_preservation: No application code paths or response shapes were changed; user-controlled HTML is still sanitized through the same wrapper with safer library behavior.

validation.notes: Dependency install completed and TypeScript server build passed. npm audit still reports unrelated vulnerabilities outside this confirmed SCA finding.

entries.residual_risk: Contextual output encoding remains required at render sites. Additional sanitize-html DoS advisories were triaged false_positive/excluded in the canonical report.

entries.fix_rationale: Upgrading jsonwebtoken safely requires coordinated JWT key rotation and code changes because the application signs RS256 tokens with an embedded legacy 1024-bit RSA key. A dependency-only upgrade risks breaking authentication or preserving insecure key settings.
entries.functionality_preservation: Authentication behavior was not changed on the SCA branch to avoid an unsafe partial migration.

validation.notes: Validated the SCA branch after the sanitizer upgrade; JWT library was intentionally deferred.

entries.residual_risk: JWT verification-bypass dependency alerts remain until maintainers approve coordinated jsonwebtoken upgrade, key rotation, and token compatibility plan.
